metadata.split_role: Expanded red/yellow, blue-staining, red-pored, and caution-lane bolete records.
metadata.notes: Physical data split only. Species record bodies were preserved; each canonical slug appears in exactly one base mushroom file. v4.2.84 updated 25 red/caution bolete records with conservative notes, sources, and explicit remaining review reasons. v4.2.86: optional photo patches are treated as fill-only for existing base-record image fields; direct image fallback hardened for Black Velvet Bolete and Suillus tomentosus where applicable. v4.2.87-r2026-04-30-mushroom-suillus-leccinum-credibility4: carried forward with patch-precedence-safe image handling. v4.2.88: 25-record mushroom credibility/review pass; porcini/Tylopilus/Xerocomus records rechecked and remaining red/caution records improved while preserving true image/local-taxonomy review flags. v4.2.96: large red-caution source split into 3 chunks of 13 records; record bodies preserved and original order retained. v4.2.97 reviewed first 25 remaining red-caution Needs Review records after the file split; stale image-only flags were cleared only where 2+ genuinely distinct real image URLs existed, one-photo/placeholder records stayed in review, taxonomy flags were preserved, and duplicate food/caution language was cleaned.

review_reason_audit.rule: Normalize explicit review signals into review_reasons/review_note and prefix notes for Needs Review list visibility.

metadata.split_note: v4.2.96 split the red-caution bolete source into three real source files. The original filename is now part 1; parts 2 and 3 continue the same record order.

review_pass_notes.rule: Clear stale image-only review only when at least two distinct real source-image URLs exist; keep one-photo and placeholder records in review.

records.culinary_uses: Some two-colored boletes are eaten, but this group is easy to confuse with faster-bluing or more suspect red/yellow boletes. Treat as experienced-level only.

mushroom_profile.summary: Minimal bolete seed entry for Bicolor Bolete. Added in the Michigan audit pass; fuller ID notes, sources, and images still need review.
staining: Pores bruise blue promptly; flesh usually faint/erratic blue
mushroom_profile.season_note: Summer and early fall under hardwoods.
research_notes: Species seed added from the Michigan bolete audit.
Needs source links and fuller field notes.
stem_feature: Red stem with yellow near apex

records.edibility_detail: Confirm red cap and stem, bright yellow pore surface, shallow tube layer, limited/erratic blue staining in flesh, and oak/hardwood setting. Avoid if it blues instantly everywhere or smells off.
records.field_identification: Red cap and mostly red stem, bright yellow pores that bruise blue, shallow tube layer, yellow flesh that usually stains blue only faintly or erratically, under hardwoods/oaks. Compare carefully with Boletus sensibilis and pseudosensibilis.

use_links.label: MushroomExpert: Boletus bicolor
use_links.notes: Red/yellow color pattern, shallow tubes, and blue bruising comparison.

records.edibility_notes: Some two-colored boletes are eaten, but this group is easy to confuse with faster-bluing or more suspect red/yellow boletes.
curation_notes: Minimal species seed. Added from the Michigan bolete audit for the clean app so the bolete section is more complete; details, seasonality, and images still need a later review. Baseline audit pass added lane, seasonality, commonness, food quality, host filtering, and comparison notes.
This record still needs species-level images and source links, but the core field-guide traits are now scaffolded for sorting and filtering.

cap_surface: Olive brown to medium brown cap
pore_color: Dark red-brown to maroon young pores

records.general_notes: Part of a difficult Brown-pored Bolete group rather than a simple duplicate. Treat species-level ID as tentative without expert review; compare cap color, pore color, staining, stem surface, and associated trees. This record represents lighter brown/yellowish cap tendency and smaller-spored concept reported in field-guide sources.
records.edibility_detail: Avoid for food; retained for ID/taxonomy comparison.
records.field_identification: Oak-associated bolete with dark reddish-brown to maroon young pores fading dull orange-brown, non-reticulate stem, and blue-to-black bruising. Often wormy and taxonomically tricky.
records.review_note: Needs review: Needs taxonomy / species-level ID check.

This record represents older Peck species concept in the brown-pored/red-staining bolete group.
records.field_identification: Brownish to yellow-brown cap, dark brown to maroon pores aging brownish yellow, blue-green bruising, and oak association. Similar to B. vermiculosoides but traditionally separated by spores/cap tones.

records.edibility_detail: Treat cautiously; confirm with good field features and local expertise.
records.mushroom_card_note: Brown Butyriboletus with yellow pores and a reticulate yellow stem that develops red zones or stains.

records.culinary_uses: Avoid; bitter Caloboletus species are not for the food list.

mushroom_profile.summary: Bitter red-stemmed Caloboletus-style bolete; not a food target and often tied up with North American name confusion.

records.edibility_detail: Not recommended; kept as a caution/comparison record.
records.mushroom_card_note: Bitter red-stemmed Caloboletus-style bolete; not a food target and often tied up with North American name confusion.
records.field_identification: Pale to brown cap, yellow pores that blue, red/pinkish reticulate stem, and bitter taste. North American use of the European name is messy.
